  "findings": [
    "Schrader valve cores were left in place during the evacuation process (SEQ).",
    "Leaving cores in during evacuation restricts flow, preventing a deep, clean vacuum and trapping moisture/non-condensables.",
    "The system is now in-service with potential moisture contamination, leading to acid formation and premature compressor degradation."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Isolate or recover the refrigerant charge according to EPA 608 guidelines.",
    "Remove the Schrader cores using a core removal tool.",
    "Evacuate the system again to under 500 microns to ensure all moisture and non-condensables are removed.",
    "Reinstall the cores, decay test, and recharge the system with clean refrigerant."
  ],
  "rationale": "Leaving Schrader cores in during evacuation severely restricts the flow rate, making it highly unlikely that a true deep vacuum (below 500 microns) was achieved throughout the entire system. This violates manufacturer specifications and standard industry practices (ACCA/EPA 608), leading to moisture retention, acid formation, and eventual system failure.",
  "references": [
    "EPA Section 608",
    "ACCA Standard 5 (HVAC Quality Installation Specification)",
    "Manufacturer Installation & Operating Instructions (Evacuation Protocols)"
  ]
}
